explanation: This is the current standard at the whale level in older servers. The idea, contrary to the test team in slot #10, is to have one win condition that you're using as a hypercarry by using four champions that buff them or keep them alive.

Standard JLD comp generally fares well in the meta. You can run this with Sinestro as a fifth for a really nice Meta Brawl team, and it's a team that is easy for F2P to build over the course of a year by focusing on a full JLD team to start.
LOD has tapered off for older server, particularly with the downfall of the physical damage meta, but this is still a stout team.
Amazons have shown good staying power in the meta if you have DMWW. Various hybrid comps can work here.

Superman Family is definitely a team that has seen its better days in PvP, but I am hopeful that this summer it may find its way back into relevancy with the introduction of a fifth team member. The biggest issues here are Krypto being broken in PvP to not use his ultimate fast enough, and Supergirl taking so long to ramp damage.

With Orphan coming to the game, I think I may move this team up above Amazons and even LOD. It's quite possible that a variation of Bat Family will be able to topple most teams moving forward, but you'll probably need DM Batman to do it.

Arkham comps are rarely seen, but Joker is still the king of Combat Cycle, and we have seen some proficiency with heavily built AMW teams in 3v3. It's a solid team for F2P to build because of the availability of non-Bleed champions.

Justice League is finally starting to get some solid comp options. I think it's still a solid intro team, and spenders and get out in front of a server by building an early version of this up to red stars. Martian Manhunter and Hawkgirl might make this an actual viable comp moving forward.

This is just an experimental theorycraft that I'd love to see people try. You get Birds of Prey and Bat Family tags with this one and I think the increased dodge and abundant healing make the team pretty stout.

Experimental team that I've been working on that should be pretty obvious for most players, but the idea in Meta Brawl is to just put your best win conditions with multiple supporters who protect your team, boost your damage output, or both.
